---
title: "Core-Periphery Structure"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Core-Periphery Structure}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

## Overview

This vignette explores the behaviour of the MFM-SBM algorithm on networks
with a core-periphery structure, where the block probability matrix does not
follow the standard assortative pattern.

## Generating a Core-Periphery Network

```{r}
library(mfmsbm)

V <- 50
K <- 2

# Core-periphery block matrix (non-assortative)
Q <- matrix(0.3, K, K)
Q[1, 1] <- 0.4
Q[2, 2] <- 0.1
Q[1, 2] <- Q[2, 1] <- 0.4

nk <- V / K
z_0 <- c(rep(1, nk), rep(2, nk))

# Generate adjacency matrix
set.seed(123)
Y <- matrix(0L, V, V)
for (row in 1:V) {
  for (col in row:V) {
    if (row != col) {
      Y[row, col] <- rbinom(1, 1, Q[z_0[row], z_0[col]])
    }
  }
}
Y <- mfmsbm:::.symmetrize_matrix(Y)
diag(Y) <- 0
```

## Running the Gibbs Sampler

```{r}
K_start <- 9
a <- b <- gamma_ <- 1
gamma_gn <- 0.5
N_iter <- 15000
burn_in <- 5000

z_start <- c(sample(1:K_start, K_start), sample(1:K_start, V - K_start, replace = TRUE))

# Gnedin prior
fit_gn <- cogibbs_gnedin(N_iter, K_start, Y, V, a, b, gamma_, gamma_gn, z_start)
cc_gn <- coclustering_matrix(fit_gn$z_post[, (burn_in + 1):N_iter])

# Poisson prior
logVn <- log_vn_miller(gamma_, V, V + 10, lambda = 1)
fit_po <- cogibbs_poisson(N_iter, K_start, Y, V, a, b, gamma_, logVn, z_start)
cc_po <- coclustering_matrix(fit_po$z_post[, (burn_in + 1):N_iter])
```

## Results

```{r}
if (requireNamespace("mcclust.ext", quietly = TRUE)) {
  memb_gn <- mcclust.ext::minVI(cc_gn, method = "avg", max.k = 20)$cl
  memb_po <- mcclust.ext::minVI(cc_po, method = "avg", max.k = 20)$cl

  cat("Gnedin K_hat:", length(unique(memb_gn)), "\n")
  cat("Poisson K_hat:", length(unique(memb_po)), "\n")
}
```
